nodes.label: Measuring Weight
nodes.description: How heavy is an object? We measure weight in grams and kilograms

nodes.label: Gram (g)
nodes.description: Used for light objects like a pencil, biscuit packet, or small fruit

nodes.label: Kilogram (kg)
nodes.description: Used for heavy objects like a bag of rice, school bag, or watermelon

nodes.label: 1 kg = 1000 g
nodes.description: Key conversion: multiply kg by 1000 to get grams

nodes.label: Measuring Capacity
nodes.description: How much liquid can a container hold?

nodes.label: Millilitre (mL)
nodes.description: Used for small amounts like medicine, a spoon of oil, or a small cup

nodes.label: Litre (L)
nodes.description: Used for large amounts like a bottle of water, bucket, or tank

nodes.label: 1 L = 1000 mL
nodes.description: Key conversion: multiply litres by 1000 to get millilitres

nodes.label: Measuring Tools
nodes.description: Tools we use for weighing and measuring liquids

nodes.label: Weighing Balance
nodes.description: Two-pan balance used by shopkeepers with standard weights

nodes.label: Measuring Cup / Jug
nodes.description: Cup with markings to measure mL or L of liquid

nodes.label: Word Problems
nodes.description: Adding, subtracting, and comparing weights and capacities

nodes.label: Add and Subtract
nodes.description: Add or subtract weights/capacities after converting to the same unit

nodes.label: Compare
nodes.description: Convert to the same unit to compare which is heavier or holds more
